skill.name: lark-event
skill.description: 飞书事件订阅：通过 WebSocket 长连接实时监听飞书事件（消息、通讯录变更、日历变更等），输出 NDJSON 到 stdout，支持 compact Agent 友好格式、正则路由、文件输出。当用户需要实时监听飞书事件、构建事件驱动管道时使用。
skill.summary: Real-time Lark event subscription via WebSocket with NDJSON output, regex routing, and agent-friendly compact format.
skill.author: larksuite

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 132 patterns but all are false positives from markdown documentation. Backticks are code formatting, '../' are relative links, and shell examples are legitimate usage documentation. The skill invokes lark-cli for read-only WebSocket event subscription with bot authentication.

low_findings.title: External CLI Dependency
low_findings.description: Skill requires lark-cli binary to be installed. Commands are executed via shell but with hardcoded arguments for legitimate Lark API operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md explicitly declares bins dependency on lark-cli - legitimate CLI tooling pattern

low_findings.title: Network WebSocket Connection
low_findings.description: Establishes WebSocket long connection to Lark servers for event subscription. Uses bot authentication with App ID and App Secret.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation clearly states read-only bot authentication pattern for official Lark API

low_findings.title: Optional File Output
low_findings.description: Supports writing events to files via --output-dir and --route parameters. File paths are user-provided but not used in shell evaluation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: File output is optional feature with standard path handling, no shell injection vectors present

content.user_title: Subscribe to Lark events in real-time
content.value_statement: Monitor Lark workspace events instantly through WebSocket connections. Build automated workflows that respond to messages, calendar changes, and contact updates without polling.
actual_capabilities: Establish WebSocket long connection to Lark event servers
Receive and output events in NDJSON or compact format
Filter events by type using --event-types parameter
Route events to different files using regex patterns
Parse and flatten IM message content for agent pipelines
Support 24 common event types in catch-all mode
limitations: Requires Lark Open Platform app configuration before use
Bot-only authentication - cannot access user-specific events
Events must be pre-registered in Lark console to be received
Single-instance lock prevents multiple subscribers per app

use_cases.title: Automated message response system
use_cases.description: Listen for incoming messages and automatically generate replies using Claude Code based on message content.
use_cases.target_user: Customer support teams building chatbot workflows

use_cases.title: Event logging and audit trail
use_cases.description: Capture all workspace events to files for compliance, analytics, or debugging purposes.
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers monitoring Lark workspace activity

use_cases.title: Cross-platform notification bridge
use_cases.description: Forward Lark events to other systems like Slack, email, or internal tools via shell pipelines.
use_cases.target_user: Integration developers connecting Lark to existing infrastructure

prompt_templates.title: Basic event subscription
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a Lark event subscription that listens for all incoming messages and outputs them in a format suitable for automated processing.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - First-time setup for message monitoring

prompt_templates.title: Filtered event routing
prompt_templates.prompt: Configure event routing to save IM messages to one directory and calendar events to another, using regex patterns to distinguish event types.

prompt_templates.title: Automated response pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a shell pipeline that reads Lark messages, sends them to Claude Code for analysis, and posts replies back to the original chat as a bot.

prompt_templates.title: Compact format for agent processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Subscribe to message events using compact output format, parse the content field with jq, and trigger actions based on message keywords.

output_examples.input: Subscribe to IM messages with compact format
Raw NDJSON output for calendar event changes
output_examples.output: Event received: calendar.calendar.event.changed_v4 - Event 'Team Meeting' updated by user_123 at 2026-04-01T10:30:00Z

best_practices: Always use --compact --quiet flags for agent pipeline integration to get clean structured output
Configure required event types and permissions in Lark Open Platform console before subscribing
Use --event-types to limit subscription to only needed events instead of catch-all mode in production
anti_patterns: Do not use --force flag unless you understand server-side event splitting across multiple connections
Avoid pretty-print JSON (--json) in high-volume pipelines as it increases output size significantly
Do not rely on catch-all mode for production - explicitly specify event types for predictable behavior

faq.question: What authentication method does this skill use?
faq.answer: Bot-only authentication using App ID and App Secret from Lark Open Platform. No user login is required.

faq.question: Can I subscribe to events dynamically without console configuration?
faq.answer: No. Events must be registered in the Lark Open Platform console under Events & Callbacks before the CLI can receive them.

faq.question: What is the difference between --event-types and --filter?
faq.answer: --event-types controls server-side subscription (which events are sent). --filter applies client-side regex filtering after events are received.

faq.question: How do I stop the event subscription?
faq.answer: Press Ctrl+C to gracefully shut down. The CLI will print the total event count received.

faq.question: Can I run multiple event subscribers simultaneously?
faq.answer: Not recommended. The single-instance lock prevents this by default. Using --force causes the server to split events randomly across connections.

faq.question: What output format should I use for Claude Code integration?
faq.answer: Use --compact --quiet for agent-friendly flattened JSON with semantic fields and noise removed.
